title: SACRAMENTO, CA—
prompt_preview:
[1 chunks] Task: Extract all distinct claims about AI or emerging technology in this press release. Do NOT assume a fixed number.
Output JSON only matching the provided schema.
Return concise, standalone claim_texts that could be tested against scientific literature. Exclude purely procedural statem

claims.claim_text: AB 2104 authorizes the California Community Colleges to establish a pilot program to allow up to 15 community college districts to offer a Bachelor of Science in nursing degree.
AB 2104 will expand the access and affordability of Bachelor of Science in nursing degrees in local communities.
The shortage of nurses in California is hurting vulnerable communities, particularly in the Central Valley.
Expanding access to nursing training programs is necessary to tackle the workforce challenge in California.
AB 2104 aims to grow the nursing workforce in areas of the state most in need.
The advancement of AB 2104 through the Higher Education Committee is important to meet California's demand for qualified nurses.
Community colleges have highly-respected faculty ready to assist in educating the next generation of nurses.
AB 2104 enhances diversity in the nursing profession, benefiting underserved communities.
Without action, the nursing shortage in California could worsen, limiting access to needed healthcare.
AB 3275 requires health plans or insurers to reimburse an uncontested claim within 10 business days for small and rural hospitals.
AB 3275 requires the Department of Managed Health Care to develop a list of claim categories to be paid within five days if care was documented.
Timely payments to small and rural hospitals are essential for their financial stability.
The closure of Madera Community Hospital highlighted the critical issue of healthcare claim reimbursement.
Small and rural hospitals rely on timely payments to operate efficiently and reduce financial vulnerability.
